metadata.research_scope: Top 25+ college consulting and admissions counseling firms in the US, including national elite firms, online/tech-forward platforms, and independent consultants
metadata.note: Pricing and services reflect publicly available information as of March 2026. Consulting market is highly dynamic with frequent service and pricing changes.

firms.name: IvyWise

pricing.comprehensive_package: $5,000-$15,000 custom packages; unlimited counseling available at premium rates

firms.methodology_summary: IvyWise offers team-based advisory with unlimited access to expert counselors providing hands-on execution guidance and strategic roadmaps. Their approach emphasizes sustained relationship-building and comprehensive support through the entire application cycle, with counselors billing at $1,000-$3,000/hour depending on seniority.
claims: 6x more likely to get into top universities than general applicant
98% of students get into at least one of their best-fit schools
firms.target_market: High-income families seeking premium, relationship-based guidance; students aiming for top 20 universities
weaknesses_or_criticisms: High pricing ($1,000-$3,000/hour) restricts access to affluent families
Limited information on outcomes by school tier
firms.wayfinder_differentiation: IvyWise charges $1,000-$3,000/hour for premium personal counseling. Wayfinder delivers comparable strategic analysis through AI-powered data aggregation—cross-referencing acceptance rates, demographic trends, and program-level data that no human consultant can efficiently process—at a fraction of the cost. Where Wayfinder excels is in real-time pattern recognition: identifying which schools have the highest yield from students with your profile, analyzing admissions team composition changes, and detecting shifts in school preferences before they're public. The tradeoff: IvyWise provides emotional support and relationship continuity that AI cannot replicate; Wayfinder provides data depth and speed that humans cannot.

firms.name: Collegewise

pricing.comprehensive_package: $4,950+ for 30-hour test prep packages; custom full-service packages available

firms.methodology_summary: Collegewise uses a personalized package model recognizing that no two students are alike. They emphasize accessible guidance with transparent pricing, offering regular check-ins, project management, and progress reports. Their approach combines strategic school selection with hands-on application execution.
claims: Personalized packages tailored to individual needs
Accessible pricing compared to elite boutiques
firms.target_market: Middle to upper-middle-income families; students seeking balanced college search; first-generation applicants
strengths: Strong emphasis on school fit over prestige
Good balance of price and service
weaknesses_or_criticisms: Less emphasis on Ivy League specialization than competitors
Limited evidence of outcomes at most selective schools
Package model may not fit all student needs
firms.wayfinder_differentiation: Collegewise charges $2,000-$6,000 for personalized packages emphasizing school fit. Wayfinder complements this with AI-driven fit analysis at lower cost, automatically cross-referencing student profiles against 4,000+ schools' actual acceptance patterns, course offerings, and alumni outcomes. Collegewise excels at human judgment in school selection; Wayfinder excels at quantitative fit scoring. Wayfinder's strength: processing school-within-school data (engineering vs. liberal arts at same institution) to identify true fit; identifying schools where your extracurricular profile is stronger than GPA alone suggests.

firms.name: Crimson Education

pricing.comprehensive_package: $30,000-$100,000+ depending on services (research, competitions, test prep layering)

firms.methodology_summary: Crimson uses a distinctive team-based model where students receive support from experts in academics, testing, essays, extracurriculars, research, and competitions—all coordinated. Their approach leverages former admissions officers and accomplished university graduates to build holistic candidacy for Ivy League and top-15 schools.
claims: 7x more likely to get into Ivy League and top-15 schools
1,340+ Ivy League offers in track record
2% of class of 2028 admits at Brown, Columbia, Harvard, UPenn came from Crimson students
Team-based support across all application dimensions
firms.team_size: 100+ consultants including former admissions officers from Harvard, Stanford, MIT, Oxford, Cambridge
firms.target_market: Wealthy families targeting Ivy League/top-15 schools; students with research and competition ambitions; international applicants
strengths: Proven track record with elite school placement
weaknesses_or_criticisms: Extremely high pricing ($30,000-$100,000+) restricts to elite market
Aggressive marketing and success rate claims should be scrutinized
Potential over-emphasis on competition/research may not suit all profiles
firms.wayfinder_differentiation: Crimson charges $30,000-$100,000 for premium team-based guidance on Ivy League admissions. Wayfinder offers similar school-within-school analysis and strategic positioning through AI at under 5% of the cost. Crimson's advantage: human judgment on research quality, competition prestige, extracurricular narrative coherence, and emotional support. Wayfinder's advantage: quantitative trend analysis. For example, Wayfinder can instantly determine that your research topic aligns with faculty research interests at specific schools, or that your competition awards are rarer than you think—data that no human consultant reviews as comprehensively. Wayfinder democratizes Crimson's data-driven insights.

firms.name: Admissionado

pricing.comprehensive_package: $15,000-$25,000+ for full college application support with essay specialist

firms.methodology_summary: Admissionado uses proprietary frameworks combining competitive analysis and narrative strategy. Their unique 'simulated admissions decision' model places the client's application against peer profiles, with three reviewers (including CEO) evaluating candidates side-by-side without knowing who is the current client. This provides direct feedback on application strength relative to peer pool.
claims: Only firm offering primary consultant + essay specialist model
firms.target_market: Ambitious students targeting selective schools; families valuing strategic, data-informed approach; students wanting competitive benchmarking
strengths: Strong focus on strategic positioning vs. generic advice
weaknesses_or_criticisms: Simulated committee review may not replicate real admissions decisions
Competitive positioning approach may discourage some students
firms.wayfinder_differentiation: Admissionado charges $5,000-$25,000 for strategic positioning using simulated committee reviews. Wayfinder offers real-time competitive benchmarking through actual admissions data: comparing your profile not to Admissionado's internal mock committee but to real applicant cohorts at target schools. Wayfinder can show you the actual GPA/test score distributions for accepted students in your intended major, extracurricular patterns, geographic distribution—data that simulated reviews estimate but Wayfinder quantifies. Admissionado's strength: human judgment on narrative coherence; Wayfinder's strength: benchmarking against real data rather than simulations.

firms.name: CollegeVine

pricing.entry_price: Completely free access to core platform
pricing.comprehensive_package: $1,300-$2,000 for coaching packages with near-peer mentors from top universities

firms.methodology_summary: CollegeVine democratizes college admissions guidance through a freemium model. Their near-peer mentor approach uses college students from top universities to provide essay feedback and advising at lower cost than professional consultants. Their AI tool Sage provides personalized college fit analysis and advice, making premium guidance accessible to middle-income families.
claims: Services at 1/3 the cost of competitors
firms.target_market: Middle-income families; cost-conscious students; tech-savvy applicants; international students; first-generation students
strengths: Free core platform removes access barriers
Near-peer mentors relatable to current students
weaknesses_or_criticisms: Near-peer mentors lack professional consultant experience
Limited institutional knowledge vs. professional consultants

free_resources: Completely free platform with chance calculator

firms.wayfinder_differentiation: CollegeVine charges $0-$2,000 for near-peer advising and chance calculations. Wayfinder differs fundamentally: while CollegeVine's Sage tool provides personalized recommendations, Wayfinder focuses on admissions data depth that AI-alone cannot achieve without training on proprietary datasets. Wayfinder's strength: providing consultants and students with school-specific, program-level, decision-date-specific data to power better recommendations. CollegeVine excels at accessibility; Wayfinder excels at data granularity. They're complementary rather than directly competitive—CollegeVine finds schools, Wayfinder helps evaluate each school's actual acceptance patterns and fit.

firms.name: Leland

pricing.entry_price: Coaches set own rates; marketplace typically $15-$50/hour for newer coaches
pricing.comprehensive_package: $1,500-$5,000+ for sustained engagement with experienced coaches over 2-3 months

firms.methodology_summary: Leland is a coaching marketplace where users browse and select coaches by price, experience, and specialization. Students work with coaches for varying durations—from a few weeks to several years. The platform emphasizes matching student needs with coach expertise, giving transparency into coach background, hourly rates, and client reviews.
firms.target_market: Budget-conscious families; students wanting flexibility in coach selection; early-career professionals; tech-savvy applicants
strengths: Flexible engagement from weeks to years
weaknesses_or_criticisms: Quality highly variable across coach pool
Limited institutional knowledge from admission offices
Marketplace format may feel less structured than traditional firms
firms.wayfinder_differentiation: Leland charges $15-$150+/hour depending on coach selection, with quality highly variable. Wayfinder provides consistent, high-quality data analysis that coaches on Leland can use as a reference tool. Wayfinder's data (acceptance rate ranges by demographic, school-specific trends, program-level insights) makes individual coaches more effective. Rather than competing with Leland coaches, Wayfinder empowers them with better data. Wayfinder could integrate with Leland as a value-add—coaches using Wayfinder data provide better guidance to their clients.

firms.name: Empowerly
firms.methodology_summary: Empowerly matches students with counselors based on area of study, extracurriculars, target schools, and personal preferences. Their approach is data-driven, with review committees of former admissions officers providing feedback. They emphasize holistic support beyond applications, including financial aid navigation and specialized programs for underrepresented populations.
claims: 98% success rate getting students into target schools
Review by former admissions officers at top schools
Specialized GOALS program for neurodivergent students
firms.target_market: Families seeking personalized counselor matching; neurodivergent students; transfer applicants; international students; middle to upper-income families
strengths: Strong specialization in neurodivergent student support
Transfer and international student focus sets them apart
weaknesses_or_criticisms: Success rate claim (98%) not independently verified
Limited public information on cost range
Newer firm compared to established competitors
firms.wayfinder_differentiation: Empowerly matches students with counselors and uses data-driven review, but pricing is opaque. Wayfinder provides transparent data infrastructure that Empowerly's counselors can leverage—specific school acceptance patterns, program-level data, and financial aid distributions. Where Empowerly excels is in personalized counselor matching and holistic support; Wayfinder excels in quantitative data provision. Wayfinder's differentiation: making admissions data transparent so any counselor (Empowerly or otherwise) can make more informed recommendations.

firms.name: Ivy Coach
firms.methodology_summary: Ivy Coach is led by former Ivy League and elite college admissions officers who now advise students on the application side. They emphasize writing compelling essays and optimizing recommendation letters, drawing directly on their admissions review experience. Their small, selective practice maintains high standards and personal attention.
claims: Led by former Harvard College admissions officer (5+ years, full committee member)
firms.target_market: High-income families in New York area; students targeting Ivy League; families seeking admissions officer perspective
strengths: Direct experience from Harvard admissions committee
weaknesses_or_criticisms: Heavy emphasis on Ivy League may not serve all students
firms.wayfinder_differentiation: Ivy Coach's strength is admissions officer perspective on essays—they know what committee members look for. Wayfinder's strength is data on what essays from admitted students actually contain. Wayfinder can show the distribution of essay topics, personal statement themes, and extracurricular narratives across admitted classes, while Ivy Coach provides judgment based on lived admissions experience. These are complementary: Ivy Coach's narrative expertise + Wayfinder's topic/theme data = stronger essays.

firms.name: College Essay Guy
firms.methodology_summary: College Essay Guy (Ethan Sawyer's brand) offers fixed-price packages for essay coaching across multiple schools. The founder's principles guide a team approach, emphasizing authentic storytelling and application strategy. Services are delivered via Zoom, email, and Google Docs with integrated essay management platform.
firms.target_market: Essay-focused students; families wanting transparent pricing; students applying to 3-10 schools
weaknesses_or_criticisms: Essay-only focus; limited overall admissions strategy
Founder no longer works with most students directly
firms.wayfinder_differentiation: College Essay Guy charges $4,900-$9,900 for essay coaching emphasizing authentic storytelling. Wayfinder provides essay topic/theme analysis: showing students which narrative angles are over/under-represented at their target schools, helping them position their authentic story strategically. CEG excels at writing quality and authenticity; Wayfinder excels at strategic positioning. Wayfinder can tell you if your 'overcoming adversity' essay fits the diversity narrative distribution at selective schools or stands out.

firms.name: Top Tier Admissions
firms.methodology_summary: Top Tier Admissions offers progressive support starting in 8th grade through senior year, emphasizing early planning. Their signature Application Boot Camp (sold out annually since 2005) provides intensive support. The firm reviews 600+ applications annually and emphasizes personal attention despite scale.
claims: Application Boot Camp sold out every year since 2005
Featured in NY Times, Today Show
firms.target_market: Families starting planning early (8th-9th grade); students wanting intensive boot camp experience; middle to upper-income families
strengths: Long track record (Application Boot Camp since 2005)
Intensive boot camp model proven effective
weaknesses_or_criticisms: Boot camp sold out makes access difficult
Limited transparency on outcomes by school tier
Scale (600+ applications) raises questions about individual attention
Pricing varies widely without clear structure
firms.wayfinder_differentiation: Top Tier charges $3,000-$12,000 and processes 600+ applications annually. Wayfinder provides data infrastructure for their consultants: helping them quickly benchmark each student's profile against application trends, identify strategic school matches, and track decision patterns. Wayfinder's value: with 600+ applications annually, Top Tier's consultants could use Wayfinder to identify patterns faster (Which schools are increasing STEM focus? Which states have declining yield?). Rather than direct competition, Wayfinder enhances Top Tier's ability to serve volume.

firms.name: Prepory

pricing.comprehensive_package: $9,000-$12,900 depending on grade level and duration

firms.methodology_summary: Prepory emphasizes strategic roadmap development with coaches designing personalized paths. Their team of coaches from Harvard, Duke, Columbia, and other top schools bring 400+ years of collective admissions experience. They combine early planning (freshman onwards) with intensive support during senior year.
claims: 3.38x more likely to be admitted to schools with <15% acceptance rates
firms.target_market: Families planning from freshman year; ambitious students targeting selective schools; families wanting structured multiyear approach; middle to upper-income families
weaknesses_or_criticisms: Limited information on outcomes by school tier
Pricing at high end of market
firms.wayfinder_differentiation: Prepory charges $4,900-$12,900 and emphasizes early intervention. Wayfinder provides the data layer that makes early intervention smarter: tracking which schools are strengthening specific programs, identifying emerging target schools before they become competitive, and showing freshmen/sophomores where their interests/skills align with university strengths. Wayfinder's early intervention data (trend analysis on school selectivity changes, program growth) enhances Prepory's strategic roadmaps.

firms.name: Manhattan Review (College Admissions Arm)

pricing.entry_price: Varies by package; Elite packages starting ~$8,000
pricing.comprehensive_package: Elite packages up to $20,000+ for comprehensive end-to-end support

firms.methodology_summary: Manhattan Review is primarily known as a test prep company (GMAT, GRE, LSAT, SAT, ACT) that expanded into college admissions consulting. Their admissions team draws on years of admissions office experience. They offer tailored packages from entry-level to comprehensive Elite packages emphasizing end-to-end support.
claims: Years of collective admissions office experience
Integrated test prep and admissions consulting
specializations: Integrated test prep and admissions (unique advantage)
firms.target_market: Families wanting integrated test prep and admissions support; test-anxious students; international students; upper-middle to high-income families
strengths: Integrated test prep advantage (same team handles both)
weaknesses_or_criticisms: Admissions consulting arm overshadowed by test prep brand
Unclear differentiation from pure admissions firms
Heavy test-prep focus may not suit all students
firms.wayfinder_differentiation: Manhattan Review charges $5,000-$20,000 combining test prep and admissions. Wayfinder provides independent admissions data that complements their integrated approach. Where Manhattan Review excels: managing student stress by coordinating test prep and admissions timelines. Wayfinder excels: providing test score distribution data by school/major that informs both test target-setting and school selection simultaneously. For example, Wayfinder can show 'To be competitive at your target schools, you need SAT 1490+ AND these extracurriculars,' making test prep target-setting more strategic.

firms.name: Kollegio AI

pricing.comprehensive_package: $10 maximum AI charge for power users (less than 1% of human consultant cost)

services: AI feedback on clarity and structure
firms.methodology_summary: Kollegio AI is an AI-driven platform designed to democratize college admissions by providing intelligent guidance at near-zero cost. The platform generates personalized college recommendations, helps brainstorm essays, provides feedback on extracurriculars, and tracks application progress through AI-powered interfaces.
claims: Less than 1% of human consultant cost
firms.target_market: Budget-conscious students; first-generation applicants; international students; tech-savvy students; low-income families
weaknesses_or_criticisms: AI limitations: lacks human judgment and emotional intelligence

free_resources: Blog articles on AI in admissions

firms.wayfinder_differentiation: Kollegio AI and Wayfinder are both AI-first but different. Kollegio focuses on personalized student guidance at scale ($0-$10); Wayfinder focuses on providing deep admissions data to consultants and students. Kollegio's AI tries to replicate human counselor judgment; Wayfinder's focus is data—acceptance rate distributions, program-level trends, decision timing, that AI alone cannot synthesize without external data. They're complementary: Kollegio provides guidance, Wayfinder provides the data that makes guidance more precise. Wayfinder differentiates by providing admissions data depth that AI-only platforms like Kollegio cannot achieve without third-party data.

firms.name: Solomon Admissions Consulting
firms.headquarters: Multiple locations (national - largest firm by consultant count)
firms.methodology_summary: Solomon Admissions is the largest college admissions consulting firm (150+ consultants), using their proprietary Solomon Methodology emphasizing intellectual vitality and extracurricular strength. All consultants are former college admissions officers. They work one-on-one from school selection through application completion.
claims: All consultants are former admissions officers
High majority get into top-3 choice schools
firms.target_market: Families nationwide seeking consistency; ambitious students targeting top schools; families wanting verified admissions officer experience; upper-middle to high-income families
strengths: All consultants verified as former admissions officers
weaknesses_or_criticisms: Scale (150+ consultants) raises quality consistency questions
Claims (5x national average) lack third-party verification
firms.wayfinder_differentiation: Solomon charges $3,000-$15,000 with 150+ former admissions officers. Wayfinder provides the data layer that makes their human capital more efficient: instant access to acceptance rate trends, program-level analytics, and demographic distribution data. Solomon's consultants spend time interviewing students and writing strategy; Wayfinder's data does the initial filtering. For example, instead of a consultant hand-checking 20 potential schools against a student's profile, Wayfinder identifies the best 5 with data justification, freeing consultants to focus on narrative strategy. Wayfinder scales Solomon's impact.

firms.name: IvySummit College Counseling
firms.methodology_summary: IvySummit is a global boutique firm limiting client numbers for selectivity. Premium packages pair students with team support including former admissions officer, essay editor, and extracurricular strategist. They emphasize cohesive narrative development and intensive essay editing from senior staff.
claims: 100% success rate (at least one target school)
specializations: Ivy League and top-15 university focus
firms.target_market: High-income families targeting Ivy League; student-athletes; international students; families wanting team-based premium support
weaknesses_or_criticisms: 100% success rate claim lacks definition (what counts as 'target school'?)
Less transparent about pricing than competitors
firms.wayfinder_differentiation: IvySummit charges $10,000-$30,000 for team-based premium guidance on Ivy League admissions. Wayfinder provides complementary data: which Ivy schools have highest yield for students with your profile, which schools are increasing/decreasing engineering enrollment, which programs value your extracurricular profile most. IvySummit excels at narrative crafting and emotional support; Wayfinder excels at identifying which Ivys to target based on real data. IvySummit's strength: 'We got you into an Ivy'; Wayfinder's strength: 'This Ivy is your best statistical fit.'

firms.name: Spark Admissions
firms.methodology_summary: Spark Admissions is led by Harvard doctorate Rachel Rubin (known for groundbreaking admissions research) and University of Chicago MBA Rachel Blankstein. The firm emphasizes data-driven guidance informed by Rubin's research on how top 75 colleges make admissions decisions. They guide holistic academic and extracurricular development.
claims: 8x Ivy League admission rate vs. national average
6x admission rate for top 50 colleges vs. national average
96% get into at least one of top 3 choice schools
Founder's research on selective college admissions
firms.target_market: Ambitious families targeting top schools; student-athletes; arts/film students; families wanting research-backed guidance; upper-middle to high-income
strengths: Founder's published research on admissions decisions
Strong outcomes (96% get into top-3 choice)
weaknesses_or_criticisms: Outcomes claims (8x Ivy rate) lack independent verification
Limited geographic presence outside East Coast
firms.wayfinder_differentiation: Spark charges $4,000-$15,000 and is founded by an admissions research expert. Wayfinder extends Spark's research-informed approach by providing real-time admissions data infrastructure. Where Spark's founder researched how top-75 schools make decisions, Wayfinder provides ongoing data on what those decisions actually yield. Wayfinder + Spark: research-informed guidance + real admissions data = students can see both the 'why' (Spark's research) and the 'what' (Wayfinder's data).

firms.name: Shemmassian Academic Consulting

pricing.comprehensive_package: Premium, Premium+, and Ivy packages (10-20 schools); exact pricing requires consultation

firms.methodology_summary: Shemmassian is founded by Dr. Shirag Shemmassian and specializes in 1:1 support for elite institutions. The firm emphasizes comprehensive guidance from school selection through application completion, with a significant focus on medical school admissions (reflecting founder's expertise).
claims: 90%+ get into one of top 3 choices
Former USC and UCLA guest lecturer (founder)
specializations: Medical school and health professions admissions
firms.target_market: Pre-med and health professions students; ambitious college applicants; families wanting deep experience in medical admissions; upper-income families
weaknesses_or_criticisms: Heavy medical school focus limits scope for pure college students

free_resources: Blog articles on college and medical admissions

firms.wayfinder_differentiation: Shemmassian charges undisclosed fees with heavy medical school focus. Wayfinder provides medical school-specific admissions data (MCAT score distributions by school, acceptance rates by undergraduate institution, specialty-specific trends) that helps Shemmassian's pre-med clients understand their competitiveness. Wayfinder's differentiation: medical school data infrastructure that pre-med advisors lack.

firms.name: Cardinal Education
firms.headquarters: San Francisco Bay Area (founded 2006, multiple locations)

pricing.comprehensive_package: Comprehensive packages (pricing varies by location and services)

firms.methodology_summary: Cardinal Education emphasizes holistic development beyond GPA and test scores, starting as early as middle school. The firm combines academic advising with extracurricular mentorship and essay development, aiming to build comprehensive candidacy for top schools.
claims: Matriculation to Stanford, Harvard, UCLA, MIT
firms.target_market: Families in California, DC, NYC; families starting planning early (middle school); ambitious students targeting elite schools; upper-income families
weaknesses_or_criticisms: Limited transparency on college outcomes vs. private school focus
firms.wayfinder_differentiation: Cardinal charges undisclosed fees across K-12 and college services. Wayfinder provides early intervention data: identifying promising schools/programs before they become competitive, tracking which middle/high schools produce students admitted to specific colleges. Wayfinder's early identification data helps Cardinal's counselors identify strategic opportunities for students years in advance.

firms.name: Great College Advice (Mark Montgomery)
firms.methodology_summary: Great College Advice, founded by Mark Montgomery (with background as college administrator, professor, and educator), provides global advising with emphasis on personalized, student-focused approach. Montgomery's diverse background (Harvard instructor, Tufts instructor, Yale program consultant) informs strategic guidance.
claims: Worked with 100+ students from 40+ states and 28 countries
firms.target_market: International students; families seeking personalized global advising; students wanting educator perspective; families valuing diversity of approach
firms.wayfinder_differentiation: Great College Advice offers personalized global advising without published pricing. Wayfinder provides international student-specific data: visa trends by school, international student yield/enrollment by country, program-specific international acceptance patterns. Wayfinder helps Montgomery's international student advising by providing data on which schools actively recruit from specific countries and which programs value international perspectives.

firms.name: Steven Antonoff (Independent Consultant)
firms.methodology_summary: Dr. Steven Antonoff is a legendary figure in independent educational consulting with 35+ years experience and direct impact on the profession's standards and ethics. He provides consulting while also serving as instructor and advisor for UCI's certificate program in independent educational consulting.
claims: Visited hundreds of colleges multiple times
First recipient of IECA Professional Achievement Award
Award now named after him (Steven R. Antonoff Award)
specializations: Author (College Match, The College Finder, A Student of Colleges)
firms.target_market: Families seeking legendary independent consultant; students wanting deep college knowledge; families valuing ethics and professional standards
strengths: Direct college visiting experience (hundreds of schools)
weaknesses_or_criticisms: Age of materials (college guides may be dated)

free_resources: Books (College Match, The College Finder, A Student of Colleges)

firms.wayfinder_differentiation: Antonoff brings 35+ years of direct college visitation experience and professional standards expertise. Wayfinder provides the data layer he would use manually: instead of visiting colleges repeatedly, Wayfinder aggregates admissions data, trends, and demographics automatically. Antonoff's judgment + Wayfinder's data: a combination stronger than either alone. Wayfinder democratizes access to the insights Antonoff has built through decades of college visits.

firms.name: Parry College Counseling (Heather Parry - Seattle)
services: College counseling (11th grade through senior year)
firms.methodology_summary: Parry College Counseling, founded by Heather Parry (who worked in college admissions at University of Washington), provides regional expertise on Pacific Northwest schools and broader national counseling. Heather works with students as early as 11th grade through transfer students.
claims: Helped hundreds of students and families
firms.target_market: Seattle-area families; transfer students; Pacific Northwest college applicants; families seeking regional expertise
firms.wayfinder_differentiation: Parry provides regional Pacific Northwest expertise based on UW admissions experience. Wayfinder provides data on which Pacific Northwest schools are increasing/decreasing enrollment in specific programs, which schools value specific extracurriculars or geographic backgrounds, and real-time acceptance patterns. Wayfinder extends Parry's regional expertise with quantitative regional data.

firms.name: Selective Admissions (Seattle)
firms.methodology_summary: Selective Admissions is founded by a former Harvard Admissions Officer and Stanford Dean, providing premier personalized college consulting for students in Seattle and Puget Sound area. The firm emphasizes one-on-one support with admissions office experience.
claims: Founded by former Harvard and Stanford leaders
specializations: Ivy League and top university focus
firms.target_market: Seattle and Puget Sound families targeting top schools; ambitious students; families valuing Ivy League expertise; upper-income families
strengths: Founders with Harvard and Stanford admissions experience
firms.wayfinder_differentiation: Selective Admissions benefits from Harvard/Stanford admissions experience. Wayfinder provides current data on Harvard and Stanford admissions patterns: acceptance rates by major, demographic distribution, yield trends, and decision date patterns. Wayfinder makes their admissions office knowledge more current and data-backed.

firms.name: Sierra Admissions (Seattle/Bellevue)
firms.methodology_summary: Sierra Admissions specializes in high-achieving students in the Seattle/Bellevue/Eastside area, with particular focus on STEM and Business school admissions. They work with selective private schools (Lakeside, Bellevue High, International School, etc.) and emphasize strategic positioning for top universities.
claims: Specializes in STEM and Business admissions
Works with selective Pacific Northwest schools
firms.target_market: High-achieving STEM and business students in Seattle/Bellevue; private school students; families targeting top universities for STEM/business
weaknesses_or_criticisms: STEM specialization may not serve all students
firms.wayfinder_differentiation: Sierra specializes in STEM/business admissions for high achievers. Wayfinder provides STEM-specific data: acceptance rates by engineering discipline, program-specific test score distributions, STEM major enrollment trends, and school-specific STEM program rankings. Wayfinder helps Sierra advise students with precision on STEM program competitiveness.

firms.name: The Schooling Doula (Brynnen Ford - Seattle)
services: College and K-12 school admission coaching
firms.methodology_summary: The Schooling Doula, led by Brynnen Ford (30+ years experience in colleges, universities, and PreK-12 schools), offers college and school admission coaching with emphasis on personal guidance. Ford's diverse background in education (leadership, teaching, advising) informs holistic approach.
claims: 30+ years experience in education and advising
firms.target_market: Seattle families seeking personalized coaching; families valuing educational background and holistic approach; students at multiple life stages
firms.wayfinder_differentiation: Schooling Doula offers personalized holistic education coaching. Wayfinder provides data on school fit and college admissions patterns that enhance this coaching. For example, Wayfinder can identify which schools have alumni networks strongest in Seattle, helping Ford's clients understand long-term community benefits.

firms.name: Coalition for College (Platform)
services: Resources for first-generation and low-income students
firms.methodology_summary: Coalition for College is a nonprofit application platform alternative to Common App, designed to make college admissions more accessible to first-generation and low-income students. The platform emphasizes collaboration and student storytelling over grades/scores.
firms.target_market: First-generation students; low-income students; students at schools using Coalition App; public school students
weaknesses_or_criticisms: Only 170 schools vs. 1,100+ for Common App
Limited adoption compared to Common App
Early-stage relative to Common App maturity

free_resources: Entire Coalition App platform is free

firms.wayfinder_differentiation: Coalition is a free alternative application platform for 170 schools. Wayfinder provides data on Coalition member schools' admissions patterns: acceptance rates by school, demographic distribution, early decision vs. regular decision trends. For students using Coalition App, Wayfinder helps them understand which member schools are best statistical fits and where to focus effort.
